step_by_step_thinking: I fully agree with the assessment. Both niacin and riboflavin are essential components of key enzyme systems that function via redox mechanisms. Specifically, nicotinamide adenine dinucleotide (NAD+) derived from niacin serves as a primary acceptor/donor of hydrogen atoms during metabolic pathways including glycolysis and the citric acid cycle - both fundamental aspects of cellular respiration which rely heavily on redox chemistry. Similarly, riboflavin forms flavin adenine dinucleotide (FAD), another vital component in various oxidoreductase activities necessary for ATP generation among other biosynthetic tasks requiring controlled oxidation states changes. These examples further reinforce how integral these two vitamins are specifically towards maintaining proper functioning under conditions governed by redox dynamics throughout biological tissues. Thus my final answer remains unchanged at option **A**.
